title: Evidence of two-dimensional flat band at the surface of antiferromagnetic kagome metal FeSn

paper_analysis.summary: This paper reports planar tunneling spectroscopy on epitaxial FeSn/Nb-doped SrTiO3 Schottky heterojunctions and identifies an anomalous conductance peak about 180 meV below the Fermi level, interpreted as evidence for a two-dimensional flat band at the kagome-terminated surface of antiferromagnetic FeSn. First-principles slab and bilayer calculations support a spin-polarized, surface-localized flat band derived from the kagome-stanene interface, while Shubnikov-de Haas measurements confirm that the film bulk retains FeSn-like electronic structure.

figures.description: Top view of the Fe kagome layer in FeSn, showing the kagome lattice unit cell.
Crystal structure of FeSn with alternating kagome Fe layers and stanene Sn layers along the c-axis.
Three-terminal planar Schottky tunneling measurement setup with tunnel, current, and reference electrodes.
I-V characteristics of FeSn/Nb:STO junctions for different Nb concentrations at 2 K.
Zero-bias differential tunneling conductance versus Nb concentration at 2 K.
Temperature-dependent I-V curves for the x = 0.5 wt.% junction; inset shows device micrograph.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV spectra for the x = 0.5 wt.% junction.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV at negative junction bias, highlighting the low-temperature upturn region.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV at positive junction bias.
Schematic of high-temperature thermionic emission and thermionic field emission across the Schottky barrier.
Schematic of low-temperature field emission tunneling across the Schottky barrier.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV spectra for the x = 0.2 wt.% junction.
Temperature-dependent dI/dV spectra for the x = 0.7 wt.% junction, with peak positions marked.
Schematic of the eight-layer antiferromagnetic FeSn slab used in calculations.
Measured dI/dV spectrum for the x = 0.7 wt.% junction at 2 K.
Calculated DOS for the kagome-terminated surface of the FeSn slab.
Calculated DOS for the Sn-terminated surface of the FeSn slab.
Calculated DOS for the bulk region of the FeSn slab.
Temperature dependence of the dI/dV peak position and full width at half maximum for the x = 0.7 wt.% junction.
Calculated bulk FeSn band structure in the antiferromagnetic phase.
Calculated band structure of the kagome-terminated surface of the FeSn slab.
Layer-resolved DOS color map for the eight-layer slab.
Orbital-projected band structure of the kagome-terminated surface.
Calculated band structure and DOS of a ferromagnetic kagome-stanene bilayer.
DOS spectra of the ferromagnetic kagome-stanene bilayer as a function of average Fe spin moment.
High-field magnetoresistance of FeSn/STO at different temperatures.
Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations extracted from magnetoresistance at different temperatures.
FFT of SdH oscillations and inset showing Lifshitz-Kosevich fit for effective mass extraction.
Field-angle-dependent SdH oscillation frequencies compared with expected Fermi-surface cross-sections.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Yes. The paper reports raw experimental measurements including I-V curves and lock-in dI/dV tunneling spectra from FeSn/Nb:STO junctions, temperature-dependent tunneling spectra, high-field magnetoresistance traces, and SdH oscillations measured in pulsed-field and DC magnet systems. It also reports structural characterization measurements from XRD, cross-sectional TEM, and EELS. The text does not specify deposited raw file formats or a public repository.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states: 'The data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.' It also states: 'The codes used to support the findings in this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.' No public repository, accession number, or downloadable dataset is mentioned in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of measurement modality and instrumentation: tunneling I-V and dI/dV acquired in a three-terminal geometry using a lock-in amplifier, voltmeter, current amplifier, and cryostat; high-field transport measured in pulsed and DC magnet systems; structural characterization by XRD, TEM, and EELS. The paper does not specify file names, formats, or a raw-data archive.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include extracted dI/dV peak positions and FWHM versus temperature, FFT spectra of SdH oscillations, LK-fit-derived effective mass, Dingle/angle-dependent frequency analysis, and model-derived quantities such as depletion width estimates and inferred surface spin moment trends. These are presented as plotted values in the figures and discussed in the text, but no tabulated dataset is described in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific source data are implied by the plotted experimental curves and calculated spectra, but the paper does not explicitly state that source data files are deposited or provided. The figures appear to combine raw/near-raw traces, extracted quantities, and simulation outputs. No explicit 'source data' statement is present in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are provided. The paper only says the codes are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental-theoretical package: experimental tunneling and transport data, structural characterization, and DFT/Wannier simulation outputs. The paper distinguishes raw measurements from derived quantities in the Methods (e.g., polynomial subtraction for magnetoresistance, FFT, LK and Dingle fits, slab DOS/band structures). However, the text does not indicate an open data archive or explicit figure-source-data files. Supplementary Notes are heavily referenced for additional analysis, but their contents are not included here.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods describe direct measurements: 'I-V characteristics', 'dI/dV spectra', 'magnetoresistance', 'SdH oscillations', and structural characterization by XRD/TEM/EELS. These are experimental raw measurements, though the paper does not say they are publicly deposited.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: The paper does not explicitly mention source data files or a source-data repository in the provided text. It only states that data and codes are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data that support the findings of this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.” “The codes used to support the findings in this study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.”
